UCS-57  IMPACT TEST SPECIMENS. (a) Impact test specimens for carbon and low alloy steels shall be of the Charpy V-notch type, prepared in accordance with this paragraph. (b) Specimen orientation shall be transverse to the principal rolling direction when the plate width permits. (c) Subsize specimens may be used when the material thickness is insufficient for full-size specimens, subject to the reduced energy requirements of UG-84. Test procedures shall comply with UCS-68. For heat-treated specimens, see UCS-85. Refer to UCS-56 for when impact testing is required. See also UCS-66 for exemption criteria.
